input.title: Food Delivery Application
input.description: Food Delivery Application is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Flutter and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Flutter, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Customer Registration and Login
requirements: Register with phone number and OTP verification
Login via phone OTP or email/password
Social login with Google
Profile with name, email, and saved addresses

FR-02.title: Restaurant Browsing
requirements: Display nearby restaurants based on user location
Filter by cuisine, rating, delivery time, and price range
Sort by distance, rating, or popularity
Show restaurant details: menu, rating, delivery time, minimum order

FR-03.title: Menu and Item Selection
requirements: Display restaurant menu with categories
Each item shows name, description, price, and image
Items can have customization options (size, toppings)
Add items to cart with quantity selection

FR-04.title: Shopping Cart and Checkout
requirements: Cart shows selected items with quantities and prices
Users can modify quantities or remove items
Apply promo codes for discounts
Select delivery address and payment method

FR-05.title: Real-time Order Tracking
requirements: Live map showing delivery driver location
Order status updates: confirmed, preparing, picked up, delivered
Estimated delivery time with real-time updates
Push notifications for status changes

FR-06.title: Payment Processing
requirements: Support credit/debit card payments
Cash on delivery option
Digital wallet integration
Payment receipt generated for each order

FR-07.title: Rating and Review
requirements: Rate restaurant with 1-5 stars after delivery
Rate delivery driver separately
Write optional text review
View restaurant ratings before ordering

FR-08.title: Restaurant Owner Dashboard
requirements: Restaurant owners can manage menu items
View and accept/reject incoming orders
Update restaurant hours and availability
View sales analytics and revenue reports

FR-09.title: Delivery Driver App
requirements: Driver receives order assignment notifications
Navigation to restaurant and customer address
Driver can mark order as picked up and delivered
Earnings tracking and daily summary

FR-10.title: Order History
requirements: View past orders with details and status
Reorder from previous orders with one tap
Download invoice for each order
Filter orders by date range

FR-11.title: Favorites and Saved Restaurants
requirements: Users can save favorite restaurants
Quick access to frequently ordered items
Favorites list accessible from profile
Suggested restaurants based on order history

FR-12.title: Customer Support Chat
requirements: In-app chat for order issues
Chat with customer support agent
Attach images of incorrect orders
Chat history saved for reference

FR-01.title: Design Customer Registration and Login UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for customer registration and login. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for customer registration and login is complete and reviewable.
The customer registration and login design is responsive and accessible.
The design for customer registration and login aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Customer Registration and Login
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for customer registration and login. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Register with phone number and OTP verification; Login via phone OTP or email/password; Social login with Google.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required customer registration and login user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for customer registration and login are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for customer registration and login is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Customer Registration and Login
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for customer registration and login. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Register with phone number and OTP verification; Login via phone OTP or email/password; Social login with Google.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for customer registration and login is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for customer registration and login enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for customer registration and login is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Customer Registration and Login
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for customer registration and login. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for customer registration and login enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for customer registration and login are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of customer registration and login satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate Customer Registration and Login with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate customer registration and login with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by customer registration and login exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for customer registration and login integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by customer registration and login are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Customer Registration and Login Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for customer registration and login. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for customer registration and login.
Security, validation, and permission checks for customer registration and login are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important customer registration and login behaviors.

FR-12.description: Implement the frontend behavior for customer support chat. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: In-app chat for order issues; Chat with customer support agent; Attach images of incorrect orders.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required customer support chat user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for customer support chat are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for customer support chat is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-12.title: Backend Implementation of Customer Support Chat
FR-12.description: Implement the backend logic for customer support chat. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: In-app chat for order issues; Chat with customer support agent; Attach images of incorrect orders.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for customer support chat is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for customer support chat enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for customer support chat is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-12.title: Database Design for Customer Support Chat
FR-12.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for customer support chat. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for customer support chat supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for customer support chat are defined correctly.
Schema changes for customer support chat can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-12.title: Testing Customer Support Chat Functionality
FR-12.description: Create and execute tests for customer support chat. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for customer support chat.
Security, validation, and permission checks for customer support chat are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important customer support chat behaviors.
